title: Review & setlist: Diana Krall brought a special kind of magic to The Cabot
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: Diana Krall performed at The Cabot in Beverly on March 28, 2026, alongside bassist John Clayton.
The 85-minute concert included song covers such as 'Almost Like Being In Love' and 'I've Got You Under My Skin'.
The performance highlighted Krall's dual talents in piano and vocals, exemplifying her jazz expertise.

title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder after allegedly stabbing Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez, 25, in Chelsea.
Incident occurred near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue; police found multiple stabbing victims, one later died at hospital.
Castellanos Cerrato pleaded not guilty, held without bail, court date set for April 21; Chelsea police seek further information.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston metro area population grew by 11,991 in 2025, the slowest since post-pandemic.
Massachusetts' statewide population rose by 15,524 in 2025, also marking the smallest increase post-pandemic.
Middlesex and Worcester counties had the largest population increases in 2025, but significantly less than in 2024.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Marine biologists in Cape Cod launched a GoFundMe to fund North Atlantic right whale conservation efforts.
The Marineland Right Whale Project has raised $630 of its $15,000 goal to support research and fieldwork.
North Atlantic right whales face extinction threats from vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss was acquired for a fourth-round pick, setting an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in a single season.
Wes Welker, traded from Miami, became one of the most successful slot receivers without winning a Super Bowl.
Josh Gordon showed potential with over 700 yards in 11 games, but off-field issues limited his time with the Patriots.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected in the eighth inning for arguing a strike call against Trevor Story.
The controversial call by umpire C.B. Bucknor was not appealed, leading to tensions between Story, Cora, and Bucknor.
Cora emphasized understanding and learning from the situation, noting the challenges involved in using the ABS system.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call, leading to Red Sox manager Alex Cora's ejection.
The controversial call happened with the Red Sox down 5-4, with Story maintaining he did not swing.
Despite Story's strikeout, the Red Sox tied the game but ultimately lost 6-5 in the 11th inning.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha has scored 11 goals and 19 points in 16 games since the Olympic break for the Bruins.
An ill-timed concussion kept Zacha from competing in the Milano-Cortina Olympics with the Czechia national team.
Zacha, acquired in a swap for Erik Haula, reached a new career-high with 26 goals in the current NHL season.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray allowed four runs over four innings in his Red Sox debut against the Cincinnati Reds.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora praised Gray's recovery after a tough first inning, noting defensive issues.
Boston's bullpen delivered solid performances, with scoreless innings by Danny Coulombe and Ryan Watson.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands attended the 'No Kings' rally at Boston Common, part of a national protest against governmental power.
Speakers included Governor Maura Healey and Senator Elizabeth Warren, addressing issues from immigration to military involvement.
Over 160 rallies occurred in Massachusetts alone, as part of 3,300 nationwide protests organized by advocacy groups.
